project.title: PATIENTS ANTIBODIES RECOGNIZED TWO ISOENZYMES OF GLYCERALDEHYDE -3P- DESHYDROGENASE OF ECHINOCOCCUS GRANULOSUS s. l. Cloning epitopes and enzymatic analysis in silico
project.projectDescription: Our group is working identifying antigens from E. granulosus s.l by immune-identification and LC-MS/MS methodologies, CE patient sera recognized antigens expressed by cell line from bovine’s protoscolices E .granulouss G1 (EGPE). The antigenic model from EGPE cells showed more sensitivity for patient’s sera reactivity than HF obtained for sheep’s E. granulosus G1  .   EGPE cells were growth in liquid media and in biphasic agarose medium forming cysts colonies .  This cellular model allows us to study expressed proteins mainly found intracellular (IC) or extracellular (EC) compartments.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Serum samples from CE patients were obtained from Hospital Municipal Ramón Santamarina, Tandil, Argentina by Dr. Jorge Gentile. Dr. Elizabeth Luz Sánchez Romaní, Laboratorio de Zoonosis Parasitaria CNSP-INS-Perú donated sera from patients with cysticercosis and fascioliasis. EGPE is a cell line obtained from bovine E. granulosus pe G1 maintained at our laboratory. Cells used for experiments were from passages 35 to 40. The cell supernatants were obtained after five-days of cell colony incubation. Cell debris was removed by centrifuging the supernatants three times (3000 rpm). Then, samples were stored in aliquots at -20°C until use. protein fractions obtained after MW cell extract separation by Sephacryl elution were identified by absorbance in a spectrophotometer (Biowave II+ , Biochrome Ltd., Cambridge, England).  by absorbance (205-280 nm). Every fraction with higher absorbance was concentrated through a 3K cut‐off membrane concentrator (Pierce, Thermo Scientific). Protein fraction and EGPE supernantant reactivity was analyzed by Western blot and using a pool of sera from 11 patients with CE with only hepatic localization. The reactive protein fractions were passed through an affinity column (Protein G HP SpinTrap - GE Healthcare) for samples and controls (sera from two patients with cysticercosis and two patients with fascioliasis). Eluted proteins were concentrated through a 3K cut‐off membrane concentrator (Pierce, Thermo Scientific) and, a 15% SDS–PAGE was performed to concentrate and clean-up protein extracts prior to in-gel digestion (data not shown). Obtained bands from Coomassie blue-stained SDS-PAGE were excised, detained, washed, reduced, alkylated and then in-gel digested with 100 ng Trypsin (Promega V5111). Peptides were recovered by elution with 50% acetonitrile- 0.5% trifluoroacetic acid, including brief sonication, and further concentrated by speed – vacuum drying. Samples were resuspended in 15 μL of water containing 0.1% formic acid, desalted using C18zip tips (Merck-millipore) , and eluted in 10 µl of H 2 O:ACN:FA (40:60:01%). The digests were analyzed by nanoLC-MS/MS in a Thermo Scientific QExactive Mass Spectrometer coupled to a nanoHPLC EASY-nLC 1000 (Thermo Scientific). For LC-MS/MS analysis, approximately two μg of peptides were loaded onto the column and eluted for 120 min using a reverse-phase column (C18, 2 µm, 100A, 50 µm x 150 mm) Easy- Spray Column PepMap RSLC (P/N ES801) suitable for separating protein complexes with a high degree of resolution. The flow rate used for the nanocolumn was 300 nL 1000-1 and the solvent range from 7% B (5 min) to 35%. The gradient duration was 120 min. Solvent A was 0.1% formic acid in water, whereas B was 0.1% formic acid in acetonitrile.. The injection volume was 2µL. The MS equipment has a high collision dissociation cell (HCS) for fragmentation and an Orbitrap analyzer (Thermoscientific, Q-Exacted). A voltage of 3.5 kV was used for Electro Spray Ionization (Thermo Scientific, EASY-SPRAY). XCalibur 3.0.63 (Thermo Scientific) software was used for data acquisition. Full-scan mass spectra was acquired in an Orbitrap analyzer. The scanned mass range was 400-1800 m/z, at a resolution of 70,000 at 400 m/z, and the twelve most intense ions in each cycle were sequentially isolated, fragmented by higher- energy collision dissociation, and measured in an Orbitrap analyzer. Peptides with a charge of +1 or with an unassigned charge state were excluded from fragmentation for MS2.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: QExactive raw data was processed using the Proteome Discoverer software (version 2.1.1.21, Thermo Scientific) and searched against the E. granulosus sequences database UniProt  with trypsin specificity and a maximum of one missed cleavage per peptide. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine residues was set as a fixed modification and oxidation of methionine was set as variable modification. Proteome Discoverer searches were performed with a precursor mass tolerance of 10 ppm and product ion tolerance of 0.05 Da. Protein hits were filtered for at least one high- confidence peptide matched with a maximum protein and peptide false discovery rate of 1%, calculated by using a reverse database strategy
